---
title: factor_analyzer.confirmatory_factor_analyzer.ConfirmatoryFactorAnalyzer
---



```python
factor_analyzer.confirmatory_factor_analyzer.ConfirmatoryFactorAnalyzer(
    specification=None,
    n_obs=None,
    is_cov_matrix=False,
    bounds=None,
    max_iter=200,
    tol=None,
    impute='median',
    disp=True,
)
```

Fit a confirmatory factor analysis model using maximum likelihood.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name          | Type                                                                                              | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | Default   |
|---------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------|
| specification | [ModelSpecification](`spotoptim.factor_analyzer.confirmatory_factor_analyzer.ModelSpecification`) | A model specification. This must be a :class:`ModelSpecification` object or ``None``. If ``None``, a :class:`ModelSpecification` object will be generated assuming that ``n_factors`` == ``n_variables``, and that all variables load on all factors. Note that this could mean the factor model is not identified, and the optimization could fail. Defaults to ``None``. | `None`    |
| n_obs         | [int](`int`)                                                                                      | The number of observations in the original data set. If this is not passed and ``is_cov_matrix`` is ``True``, then an error will be raised. Defaults to ``None``.                                                                                                                                                                                                          | `None`    |
| is_cov_matrix | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                    | Whether the input ``X`` is a covariance matrix. If ``False``, assume it is the full data set. Defaults to ``False``.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | `False`   |
| bounds        | list of tuples                                                                                    | A list of minimum and maximum boundaries for each element of the input array. This must equal ``x0``, which is the input array from your parsed and combined model specification. The length is: ((n_factors * n_variables) + n_variables + n_factors + (((n_factors * n_factors) - n_factors) // 2). If ``None``, nothing will be bounded. Defaults to ``None``.          | `None`    |
| max_iter      | [int](`int`)                                                                                      | The maximum number of iterations for the optimization routine. Defaults to 200.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | `200`     |
| tol           | [float](`float`)                                                                                  | The tolerance for convergence. Defaults to ``None``.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | `None`    |
| disp          | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                    | Whether to print the scipy optimization ``fmin`` message to standard output. Defaults to ``True``.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | `True`    |

## Raises {.doc-section .doc-section-raises}

| Name   | Type                       | Description                                                      |
|--------|----------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | [ValueError](`ValueError`) | If ``is_cov_matrix`` is ``True``, and ``n_obs`` is not provided. |

## Attributes {.doc-section .doc-section-attributes}

| Name            | Type                                                                                              | Description                                                             |
|-----------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| model           | [ModelSpecification](`spotoptim.factor_analyzer.confirmatory_factor_analyzer.ModelSpecification`) | The model specification object.                                         |
| loadings_       | [numpy](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`)                                                       | The factor loadings matrix. ``None``, if ``fit()`` has not been called. |
| error_vars_     | [numpy](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`)                                                       | The error variance matrix.                                              |
| factor_varcovs_ | [numpy](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`)                                                       | The factor covariance matrix.                                           |
| log_likelihood_ | [float](`float`)                                                                                  | The log likelihood from the optimization routine.                       |
| aic_            | [float](`float`)                                                                                  | The Akaike information criterion.                                       |
| bic_            | [float](`float`)                                                                                  | The Bayesian information criterion.                                     |

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}


::: {#c6cfa971 .cell execution_count=1}
``` {.python .cell-code}
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import os
from spotoptim.factor_analyzer import (ConfirmatoryFactorAnalyzer,
                                      ModelSpecificationParser)
from spotoptim.utils import get_internal_datasets_folder
X = pd.read_csv(os.path.join(get_internal_datasets_folder(), 'test11.csv'))
model_dict = {"F1": ["V1", "V2", "V3", "V4"],
              "F2": ["V5", "V6", "V7", "V8"]}
model_spec = ModelSpecificationParser.parse_model_specification_from_dict(X, model_dict)
cfa = ConfirmatoryFactorAnalyzer(model_spec, disp=False)
cfa = cfa.fit(X.values)
print(np.round(cfa.loadings_, 2))
# array([[0.99, 0.  ],
#        [0.46, 0.  ],
#        [0.35, 0.  ],
#        [0.58, 0.  ],
#        [0.  , 0.99],
#        [0.  , 0.73],
#        [0.  , 0.38],
#        [0.  , 0.5 ]])

print(np.round(cfa.factor_varcovs_, 2))
# array([[1.  , 0.17],
#        [0.17, 1.  ]])

loadings_se, variances_se = cfa.get_standard_errors()
print(np.round(loadings_se, 2))
# array([[0.07, 0.  ],
#        [0.04, 0.  ],
#        [0.04, 0.  ],
#        [0.05, 0.  ],
#        [0.  , 0.06],
#        [0.  , 0.05],
#        [0.  , 0.04],
#        [0.  , 0.04]])

print(np.round(variances_se, 2))
# array([0.12, 0.05, 0.05, 0.06, 0.1 , 0.07, 0.05, 0.05])

print(np.round(cfa.transform(X.values), 2))
# array([[-0.47, -1.09],
#        [ 2.59,  1.2 ],
#        [-0.47,  2.66],
#        ...,
#        [-1.59, -0.92],
#        [ 0.19,  0.88],
#        [-0.28, -0.77]])
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
[[0.99 0.  ]
 [0.46 0.  ]
 [0.35 0.  ]
 [0.58 0.  ]
 [0.   0.99]
 [0.   0.73]
 [0.   0.38]
 [0.   0.5 ]]
[[1.   0.17]
 [0.17 1.  ]]
[[0.07 0.  ]
 [0.04 0.  ]
 [0.04 0.  ]
 [0.05 0.  ]
 [0.   0.06]
 [0.   0.05]
 [0.   0.04]
 [0.   0.04]]
[0.12 0.05 0.05 0.06 0.1  0.07 0.05 0.05]
[[-0.47 -1.09]
 [ 2.59  1.2 ]
 [-0.47  2.66]
 ...
 [-1.59 -0.92]
 [ 0.19  0.88]
 [-0.28 -0.77]]
```
:::
:::


## Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [fit](#spotoptim.factor_analyzer.confirmatory_factor_analyzer.ConfirmatoryFactorAnalyzer.fit) | Perform confirmatory factor analysis. |
| [get_model_implied_cov](#spotoptim.factor_analyzer.confirmatory_factor_analyzer.ConfirmatoryFactorAnalyzer.get_model_implied_cov) | Get the model-implied covariance matrix (sigma) for an estimated model. |
| [get_standard_errors](#spotoptim.factor_analyzer.confirmatory_factor_analyzer.ConfirmatoryFactorAnalyzer.get_standard_errors) | Get standard errors from the implied covariance matrix and implied means. |
| [transform](#spotoptim.factor_analyzer.confirmatory_factor_analyzer.ConfirmatoryFactorAnalyzer.transform) | Get the factor scores for a new data set. |

### fit { #spotoptim.factor_analyzer.confirmatory_factor_analyzer.ConfirmatoryFactorAnalyzer.fit }

```python
factor_analyzer.confirmatory_factor_analyzer.ConfirmatoryFactorAnalyzer.fit(
    X,
    y=None,
)
```

Perform confirmatory factor analysis.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name   | Type                              | Description                                                                                                                             | Default    |
|--------|-----------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| X      | [array](`array`) - [like](`like`) | The data to use for confirmatory factor analysis. If this is just a covariance matrix, make sure ``is_cov_matrix`` was set to ``True``. | _required_ |
| y      | [ignored](`ignored`)              | Ignored.                                                                                                                                | `None`     |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                                                                                              | Description                                     |
|--------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------|
| self   | [ConfirmatoryFactorAnalyzer](`spotoptim.factor_analyzer.confirmatory_factor_analyzer.ConfirmatoryFactorAnalyzer`) | The fitted confirmatory factor analyzer object. |

#### Raises {.doc-section .doc-section-raises}

| Name   | Type                               | Description                                                               |
|--------|------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | [ValueError](`ValueError`)         | If the specification is not None or a :class:`ModelSpecification` object. |
|        | [AssertionError](`AssertionError`) | If ``is_cov_matrix`` was ``True`` and the matrix is not square.           |
|        | [AssertionError](`AssertionError`) | If ``len(bounds)`` != ``len(x0)``                                         |

#### Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

::: {#4a269070 .cell execution_count=2}
``` {.python .cell-code}

model_dict = {"F1": ["V1", "V2", "V3", "V4"],
              "F2": ["V5", "V6", "V7", "V8"]}
model_spec = ModelSpecificationParser.parse_model_specification_from_dict(X, model_dict)
cfa = ConfirmatoryFactorAnalyzer(model_spec, disp=False)
cfa = cfa.fit(X.values)
print(np.round(cfa.loadings_, 2))
# array([[0.99, 0.  ],
#        [0.46, 0.  ],
#        [0.35, 0.  ],
#        [0.58, 0.  ],
#        [0.  , 0.99],
#        [0.  , 0.73],
#        [0.  , 0.38],
#        [0.  , 0.5 ]])
```

### get_model_implied_cov { #spotoptim.factor_analyzer.confirmatory_factor_analyzer.ConfirmatoryFactorAnalyzer.get_model_implied_cov }

```python
factor_analyzer.confirmatory_factor_analyzer.ConfirmatoryFactorAnalyzer.get_model_implied_cov(
)
```

Get the model-implied covariance matrix (sigma) for an estimated model.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name              | Type                                        | Description                          |
|-------------------|---------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------|
| model_implied_cov | [numpy](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`) | The model-implied covariance matrix. |

#### Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

::: {#94c27cc5 .cell execution_count=3}
``` {.python .cell-code}
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import os
from spotoptim.factor_analyzer import (ConfirmatoryFactorAnalyzer,
                                      ModelSpecificationParser)
from spotoptim.utils import get_internal_datasets_folder
X = pd.read_csv(os.path.join(get_internal_datasets_folder(), 'test11.csv'))
model_dict = {"F1": ["V1", "V2", "V3", "V4"],
              "F2": ["V5", "V6", "V7", "V8"]}
model_spec = ModelSpecificationParser.parse_model_specification_from_dict(X, model_dict)
cfa = ConfirmatoryFactorAnalyzer(model_spec, disp=False)
cfa = cfa.fit(X.values)
print(np.round(cfa.get_model_implied_cov(), 2))
# array([[2.08, 0.46, 0.35, 0.58, 0.17, 0.13, 0.06, 0.09],
#        [0.46, 1.17, 0.16, 0.27, 0.08, 0.06, 0.03, 0.04],
#        [0.35, 0.16, 1.07, 0.2 , 0.06, 0.04, 0.02, 0.03],
#        [0.58, 0.27, 0.2 , 1.29, 0.1 , 0.07, 0.04, 0.05],
#        [0.17, 0.08, 0.06, 0.1 , 2.04, 0.72, 0.37, 0.49],
#        [0.13, 0.06, 0.04, 0.07, 0.72, 1.48, 0.28, 0.37],
#        [0.06, 0.03, 0.02, 0.04, 0.37, 0.28, 1.12, 0.19],
#        [0.09, 0.04, 0.03, 0.05, 0.49, 0.37, 0.19, 1.29]])
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
[[2.08 0.46 0.35 0.58 0.17 0.13 0.06 0.09]
 [0.46 1.17 0.16 0.27 0.08 0.06 0.03 0.04]
 [0.35 0.16 1.07 0.2  0.06 0.04 0.02 0.03]
 [0.58 0.27 0.2  1.29 0.1  0.07 0.04 0.05]
 [0.17 0.08 0.06 0.1  2.04 0.72 0.37 0.49]
 [0.13 0.06 0.04 0.07 0.72 1.48 0.28 0.37]
 [0.06 0.03 0.02 0.04 0.37 0.28 1.12 0.19]
 [0.09 0.04 0.03 0.05 0.49 0.37 0.19 1.29]]
```
:::
:::


### get_standard_errors { #spotoptim.factor_analyzer.confirmatory_factor_analyzer.ConfirmatoryFactorAnalyzer.get_standard_errors }

```python
factor_analyzer.confirmatory_factor_analyzer.ConfirmatoryFactorAnalyzer.get_standard_errors(
)
```

Get standard errors from the implied covariance matrix and implied means.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                                                                                          | Description                                                                                                                                               |
|--------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| tuple  | [Tuple](`typing.Tuple`)\[[np](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`), [np](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`)\] | - loadings_se (numpy.ndarray): The standard errors for the factor loadings. - error_vars_se (numpy.ndarray): The standard errors for the error variances. |

#### Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

::: {#ab11a6c6 .cell execution_count=4}
``` {.python .cell-code}
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import os
from spotoptim.factor_analyzer import (ConfirmatoryFactorAnalyzer,
                                      ModelSpecificationParser)
from spotoptim.utils import get_internal_datasets_folder
X = pd.read_csv(os.path.join(get_internal_datasets_folder(), 'test11.csv'))
model_dict = {"F1": ["V1", "V2", "V3", "V4"],
              "F2": ["V5", "V6", "V7", "V8"]}
model_spec = ModelSpecificationParser.parse_model_specification_from_dict(X, model_dict)
cfa = ConfirmatoryFactorAnalyzer(model_spec, disp=False)
cfa = cfa.fit(X.values)
loadings_se, variances_se = cfa.get_standard_errors()
print(np.round(loadings_se, 2))
# array([[0.07, 0.  ],
#        [0.04, 0.  ],
#        [0.04, 0.  ],
#        [0.05, 0.  ],
#        [0.  , 0.06],
#        [0.  , 0.05],
#        [0.  , 0.04],
#        [0.  , 0.04]])

print(np.round(variances_se, 2))
# array([0.12, 0.05, 0.05, 0.06, 0.1 , 0.07, 0.05, 0.05])
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
[[0.07 0.  ]
 [0.04 0.  ]
 [0.04 0.  ]
 [0.05 0.  ]
 [0.   0.06]
 [0.   0.05]
 [0.   0.04]
 [0.   0.04]]
[0.12 0.05 0.05 0.06 0.1  0.07 0.05 0.05]
```
:::
:::


### transform { #spotoptim.factor_analyzer.confirmatory_factor_analyzer.ConfirmatoryFactorAnalyzer.transform }

```python
factor_analyzer.confirmatory_factor_analyzer.ConfirmatoryFactorAnalyzer.transform(
    X,
)
```

Get the factor scores for a new data set.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name   | Type                              | Description                                                                             | Default    |
|--------|-----------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| X      | [array](`array`) - [like](`like`) | The data to score using the fitted factor model, shape (``n_samples``, ``n_features``). | _required_ |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                        | Description                                                         |
|--------|---------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|
| scores | [numpy](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`) | The latent variables of X, shape (``n_samples``, ``n_components``). |

#### Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

::: {#3f6098e9 .cell execution_count=5}
``` {.python .cell-code}
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import os
from spotoptim.factor_analyzer import (ConfirmatoryFactorAnalyzer,
                                      ModelSpecificationParser)
from spotoptim.utils import get_internal_datasets_folder
X = pd.read_csv(os.path.join(get_internal_datasets_folder(), 'test11.csv'))
model_dict = {"F1": ["V1", "V2", "V3", "V4"],
              "F2": ["V5", "V6", "V7", "V8"]}
model_spec = ModelSpecificationParser.parse_model_specification_from_dict(X, model_dict)
cfa = ConfirmatoryFactorAnalyzer(model_spec, disp=False)
cfa = cfa.fit(X.values)
print(np.round(cfa.transform(X.values), 2))
# array([[-0.47, -1.09],
#        [ 2.59,  1.2 ],
#        [-0.47,  2.66],
#        ...,
#        [-1.59, -0.92],
#        [ 0.19,  0.88],
#        [-0.28, -0.77]])
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
[[-0.47 -1.09]
 [ 2.59  1.2 ]
 [-0.47  2.66]
 ...
 [-1.59 -0.92]
 [ 0.19  0.88]
 [-0.28 -0.77]]
```
:::
:::
